author: @simonexperiences
text:
encore une bonne vidéo! je cherche à comprendre certaines chose sur le noyau magnétique des générateurs, avantageux ou pas?
certains sont parallèles d'autres diagonales ou obliques... laminés, pleins... les generateurs axiaux n'en ont pas... comment avoir le moins de cogging possible sans pour autant sacrifier les performances? je suis adepte de google scholar mais j'aimerai bien une petite ' vulgarisation ' sur le sujet si ça vous dit bien sûr! ;-)

author: @judyofthewoods
text: Great piece of kit. I have an old British army 12V field generator (for telephones, I was told) which has a single hand crank. Still working. Two cranks would be better, and still better to pedal it - more strength, less fatigue, and leaves your hands free. I had an engineering workshop fabricate a  cam-type crank for it and I put the open end into a bearing in a wooden upright, all mounted on a board.

author: @Marian_Martin_Morgenstern
text:
you could add a "simple" bench style seat to make it a bit more comfortable to use. just some board around 8-10 inch an up to 2 feet long with a rod as a leg. just glue 2 3-4 long inch pieces of cut of under the seat part, drill a hole with a hole-saw in the cut-offs and the board with the diameter of the stave you use… and if you don't have round stock… you do have a lathe
glue everything together so that the holes align with each other and hammer that round stock in (or you can be gentle and use glue) xD

author: @MichaelSkinner-e9j
text:
Oh don’t worry about your height or anything else.  You are just fine.

Us younger guys need you and others in your generation to be around. You have seen a lot more and your intelligence is appreciated not just in the chemical side, but practical and common sensical side.

If there’s one thing that’s sorely lacking in the real world, is logic and common sense, especially when it comes to chaotic matters- which necessitates an even hand.

replies.author: @johnbarry8185
replies.text:
THAT was refreshing to read.  For a young man(I assume) to STATE that he NEEDS  the wisdom of older people, is wisdom itself.  I don't know you, but I am SO proud of you.  My children are the same way, but they are a small school of fish in a vast ocean.  With that attitude, you will do very well in life, young man.
Carry on, and Godspeed.
